title: Treehouse Bed
summary: A twin bed frame built to look like a peaked-roof treehouse cabin, with real board-and-batten siding and a shingled roof — a genuine themed structure, not just a decorated bed frame.
description:
This is a real structure built around a twin mattress rather than a themed headboard bolted onto a plain frame — angled 2x4 legs form a peaked roofline, sided in 1x8 boards, and roofed with overlapping 1x8 roofing boards laid working out from the center. Lay out the front and back frames first, being precise about the roof angles at the top of the legs and confirming both the top and bottom of the legs measure exactly 75" wide — **this width has to match at both top and bottom**, or the whole structure develops a lean once the roof trim and siding go on.

Once both end frames stand, the roof-angled trim ties them together, and the remaining roof trim attaches to the fronts of the legs — **keep the front roof trim low enough that the roofing boards added later will lay flat along the marked line**, a detail easy to get wrong if you eyeball the trim height instead of checking it against the plan's reference line.

Siding goes on starting from the bottom row and working up, checking square and level as you go, since an early siding board that's slightly off will compound by the time you reach the roofline. The roof itself is shingled from the center out — **work from the center roofing board outward in both directions**, screwing carefully into the front 1x6 roofing trim underneath, since a screw that misses this trim board won't hold. Mattress slats go in last, spaced roughly 5" apart, with the plan explicitly noting to add more slats than shown if your particular mattress needs firmer support.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Long point measurement, one end cut at 10° bevel.
Long point measurement, one end cut at 10° bevel.
Long point to short point, both ends cut 10° off square, ends parallel.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two back legs at 75", three front legs at 67-7/8", two mattress cleats at 75", one ladder step at 31-3/4" and nine mattress slats at 39" from the 2x4, 8 ft or stud length. Cut two side roof trim at 42-5/8" and two front/back roof trim at 76-1/2" from the 1x6. Cut eight side siding at 42", four front siding at 47-1/2", four small front siding at 4-1/4", one front mattress trim at 24-3/4" and eleven roof boards at 45-3/4" from the 1x8. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to twelve 8 ft 2x4, 8 ft or stud length, three 8 ft 1x6 and twelve 8 ft 1x8. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the front and back frames
steps.body: Lay out the front and back frames carefully and attach the mattress cleats, keeping the roof angles right at the tops of the legs. Confirm the tops of the legs measure 75" wide, matching the bottom.

steps.title: Join the frames with roof trim
steps.body: Attach the front frame to the back frame with the angled roof trim.

steps.title: Add the remaining roof trim
steps.body: Attach the remaining roof trim to the fronts of the legs and the back. Keep the front trim low enough that the roofing boards will lay flat along the reference line.

steps.title: Attach the siding
steps.body: Attach the bottom row of side siding first, checking square and level, then work upward to complete the siding on all sides.

steps.title: Add the mattress trim and ladder step
steps.body: Add the front mattress trim and the ladder step, centering the step vertically on the height of the bed.

steps.title: Attach the roof
steps.body: Attach the center roof board first, screwing carefully into the front 1x6 roofing trim. Working from the center outward, attach the remaining roof boards.

steps.title: Add the mattress slats
steps.body: Lay the 2x4 slats inside the bed on the mattress cleats, spaced about 5" apart, and attach with screws. Add more slats if your mattress requires firmer support.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Twin bed built to look like a peaked-roof treehouse cabin
